pastPositive.title: Past-positive
pastPositive.high: You draw warmth and stability from positive memories and traditions. This is a real resource — a groundedness many people lack.
pastPositive.mid: You revisit your past selectively and mostly warmly, without living in it.
pastPositive.low: You reach little for positive memory or tradition; you may be missing a stable base many draw on under pressure.

pastNegative.title: Past-negative
pastNegative.high: Regret and painful recall intrude on you more than they should. This does not make you wrong — but it is worth asking what those replays are teaching you that you have not yet learned.
pastNegative.mid: You carry some regret but do not live there.
pastNegative.low: You are largely free of regret-driven replays — a strength, as long as it is not avoidance of real lessons.

presentHedonistic.title: Present-hedonistic
presentHedonistic.high: You go toward stimulation and pleasure in the moment. This is vitality — it risks underweighting the future, but the future also needs people who are alive right now.
presentHedonistic.mid: You enjoy the present without making it your whole horizon.
presentHedonistic.low: You are sparing with present pleasure — fine if chosen, costly if it leaves you brittle.

presentFatalistic.title: Present-fatalistic
presentFatalistic.high: You feel much is out of your hands and planning barely helps. Sometimes accurate, sometimes a story that quietly disables you. Worth asking which situations each one really is.
presentFatalistic.mid: You accept what you cannot change without generalizing it.
presentFatalistic.low: You hold a strong sense of agency over the present — protect it from becoming denial of real constraints.

future.title: Future-oriented
future.high: You plan, defer gratification, and aim years ahead. This is a powerful mode — ensure the plan you are deferring toward is one you actually want to arrive at.
future.mid: You hold a future horizon without living only for it.
future.low: You hold a short horizon toward the future. Fine if chosen; costly if it leaves long things unmade.

balanceNote: There is no “balanced” profile prescribed here. A leaning future with warmth for the past and room for present pleasure tends to be resilient; but a dominant future with a starved present, or a dominant present with an unbuilt future, each has a predictable cost.

shareTexts.invite: How do you relate to past, present, and future?
shareTexts.minimal: I framed my Time Horizon Portrait. What is yours?

recommendation.label: Outfinity initiative
recommendation.title: Explorer Circle
recommendation.text: Explorer Circle is a community for builders comparing their time horizon and finding partners whose orientations complement across a long-horizon mission. You do not need to share your result to participate.
recommendation.note: Opening Explorer Circle takes you to Substack. Your result is not transmitted.
